GlobalPolicy.module-level-focus: Fast deterministic checks for vvoc schema v3 layout, role parsing, `vv-role:*` resolution, managed default-agent/command registration, managed agent prompt scaffolding, managed agent registration, role preset application, controller main-session system guidance injection, hash-anchored editing, secrets redaction, plugin toggle config and CLI, work-item protocol parsing, resumable workflow result repair, workflow state transitions, launch enforcement, review-only result parsing, loop gating, and work-item state persistence (hydrate/snapshot/round-trip).
GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Typecheck, lint, format-check, and test the merged CLI + plugin surface after each coordinated role-system or workflow change.
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Buildable package output plus stable command behavior for installation, managed default controller registration, role management, role resolution, preset switching, primary-session system guidance, hash-anchored editing, Guardian review, secrets redaction, plugin runtime toggle, tracked subagent rename, review-only workflows, deterministic workflow enforcement, and per-session work-item state persistence across process restarts and session switches.
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log secrets, credentials, raw tokens, or high-risk payloads outside the existing Guardian truncation policy.

VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.NAME: PluginInstall
VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.scenario: User runs `vvoc init` or `vvoc install` to bootstrap the package into a fresh global or project config root.
VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.expected-outcome: The pinned vvoc package specifier is present in the resolved OpenCode `plugin` array, `default_agent` is `vv-controller`, the managed controller/enhancer agents and managed subagents are registered in OpenCode config with `vv-role:*` model references, managed `/vv-plan` and `/vv-review` command definitions exist, managed OpenCode config sets `tools.apply_patch = false`, managed prompts exist in the scope-correct agents directory, and the canonical `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json` file is fully seeded with built-in roles and built-in role presets.

required-signals.trace-sequence: resolvePaths -> ensurePackageInstalled -> syncManagedAgentRegistrations -> installManagedAgentPrompts -> installVvocConfig -> status reports configured role-based setup

VF-CONFIG-SYNC.NAME: ManagedConfigSync
VF-CONFIG-SYNC.scenario: User syncs managed vvoc config after changing package versions or moving to another device.
VF-CONFIG-SYNC.expected-outcome: Canonical vvoc schema v3 and managed prompt files are rewritten canonically, the managed default controller, command definitions, enhancer/subagent registrations stay present in OpenCode config with canonical values and `vv-role:*` references, managed OpenCode config keeps `tools.apply_patch = false`, managed `vv-*` presets are refreshed to vvoc defaults, current explicit role-based values survive the vvoc.json rewrite where applicable, and pre-role vvoc schemas fail loudly instead of being migrated.

required-signals.trace-sequence: inspect canonical config -> parse current role values -> render canonical JSON -> syncManagedAgentRegistrations

VF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.NAME: RoleManagement
VF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.scenario: User runs `vvoc role list`, `vvoc role set`, or `vvoc role unset`.
VF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.expected-outcome: Built-in roles are always shown, custom roles can be created or removed, concrete assignments are normalized as `provider/model[:variant]`, and built-in roles cannot be removed accidentally.

required-signals.trace-sequence: read canonical vvoc.json -> validate role id -> normalize assignment -> write role map or render inventory

VF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.NAME: RoleResolution
VF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.scenario: OpenCode loads config that contains `model: vv-role:<role-id>` in supported root, agent, or command fields.
VF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.expected-outcome: Supported fields resolve to concrete model selections at startup, variant-bearing assignments split into native `model` plus `variant` fields where supported, and unknown roles fail loudly with a field-specific error.

required-signals.trace-sequence: load canonical role map -> inspect model-bearing config fields -> resolve `vv-role:*` values -> mutate OpenCode config object or throw explicit error

VF-PRESET-SWITCH.NAME: PresetSwitch
VF-PRESET-SWITCH.scenario: User applies a named preset such as `vvoc preset vv-openai` or `vvoc preset vv-zai`.
VF-PRESET-SWITCH.expected-outcome: Only the roles declared in the selected preset change, unlisted roles remain untouched, the canonical vvoc role map is updated, and OpenCode config stays role-reference based rather than being rewritten per preset.

required-signals.trace-sequence: read canonical vvoc.json -> resolve preset -> write listed role assignments -> print per-role summary

VF-HASHLINE-EDIT.NAME: HashlineEdit
VF-HASHLINE-EDIT.scenario: User reads a text file and then edits it through the default-on hash-anchored `edit` override.
VF-HASHLINE-EDIT.expected-outcome: `read` output is rewritten to `line#hash#anchor|content` using full-snapshot context when available, anchored edits succeed against the current snapshot, stale anchors fail loudly with refreshed anchors, and BOM/CRLF are preserved when writing back.

required-signals.trace-sequence: tool.execute.after(read) -> hashline rows emitted -> edit anchor validation -> batch edit application -> metadata filediff emitted

VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.NAME: GuardianReview
VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.scenario: OpenCode asks for a permission decision while Guardian is active.
VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.expected-outcome: Guardian resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role, low-risk actions may be auto-approved, and higher-risk actions fall back to manual approval.

VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.NAME: SystemContextInjection
VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.scenario: User starts a primary chat session with vvoc installed.
VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.expected-outcome: The main-session user message carries reusable vvoc system guidance for proactive context gathering, an editing workflow that prefers `read` plus the hashline-backed `edit` tool over shell rewrites, standard trajectories, task routing, compact working state, reroute-on-evidence, semantic continuity, assumption discipline, anti-drift budgeting, and project-overlay precedence, while known subagents including vv-analyst and vv-architect do not inherit that guidance.

required-signals.trace-sequence: chat.message -> resolved primary agent -> append message.system guidance -> skip known subagents

VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.NAME: SecretsRedaction
VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.scenario: LLM request is sent with secrets in message history, tool inputs, or tool outputs.
VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.expected-outcome: Secrets are replaced with `__VVOC_SECRET_*` placeholders before LLM request, restored after LLM response, and restored before tool execution.

VF-RENAME-AGENTS.NAME: TrackedAgentRename
VF-RENAME-AGENTS.scenario: User runs vvoc install or vvoc sync after the vv-* rename lands.
VF-RENAME-AGENTS.expected-outcome: Managed agent registrations and prompt file references use vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer. Prompt lookup works for renamed files. Model-roles bindings use vv-* keys. System-context-injection excludes vv-* names from primary-session guidance.

required-signals.trace-sequence: syncManagedAgentRegistrations -> vv-* agent names and prompt refs written -> installManagedAgentPrompts for vv-* file names

VF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.NAME: WorkItemTools
VF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.scenario: Main session calls work_item_open, work_item_list, or work_item_close.
VF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.expected-outcome: Items are created with deterministic IDs, same key plus same title returns same ID, same key plus different title fails, list reflects current state, and close is explicit.

required-signals.trace-sequence: work_item_open -> idempotent create or return -> work_item_list -> state reflects open items -> work_item_close -> state reflects closed

VF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.NAME: LaunchEnforcement
VF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.scenario: Main session launches a tracked subagent through the task tool.
VF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.expected-outcome: Tracked launches without valid VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID header are rejected, unknown work items are rejected, invalid route-specific transitions are blocked, review-only fresh work items may launch vv-spec-reviewer or vv-code-reviewer directly, round-limit overflow is rejected, and untracked launches pass through unaffected.

required-signals.trace-sequence: tool.execute.before(task) -> inspect subagent_type -> if tracked: validate header + work item + transition + round limit

VF-RESULT-PARSING.NAME: ResultParsing
VF-RESULT-PARSING.scenario: A tracked subagent completes and returns output.
VF-RESULT-PARSING.expected-outcome: Parsed results update workflow state correctly for implementation and review-only workflows, OpenCode task wrapper metadata is stripped before strict subagent result parsing, malformed resumable tracked outputs get at most one same-session repair attempt, NEEDS_CONTEXT and BLOCKED halt workflow, malformed results otherwise fail closed, and counters increment deterministically.

required-signals.trace-sequence: tool.execute.after(task) -> normalize recognized OpenCode task wrapper -> parse top block -> if malformed and resumable: attempt one same-session repair -> validate VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID + VVOC_STATUS -> update state and counters -> halt on protocol error or hard stop

VF-LOOP-GATE.NAME: LoopGate
VF-LOOP-GATE.scenario: A tracked subagent review cycle repeats after implementation fixes.
VF-LOOP-GATE.expected-outcome: Rounds 1 and 2 are allowed, round 3 is rejected with explicit next-action guidance, no semantic heuristics are used, and main session can inspect state and recover.

required-signals.trace-sequence: compute reviewRound -> check against MAX_REVIEW_ROUNDS -> block or allow with clear guidance

VF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.NAME: WorkflowGuidance
VF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.scenario: Main session starts a chat with WorkflowPlugin active.
VF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.expected-outcome: Eligible orchestrating primary agents including vv-controller receive workflow protocol guidance, including the header-first plus lightweight tagged assignment-body convention for tracked prompts. Helper primaries such as enhancer and known subagents including vv-analyst, vv-architect, vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer do not inherit it.

required-signals.trace-sequence: chat.message -> resolved primary agent -> append workflow system instruction -> skip known subagents

VF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.NAME: PluginToggle
VF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.scenario: User enables or disables a vvoc-managed plugin via `vvoc plugin enable|disable ` or by directly editing vvoc.json.
VF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.expected-outcome: The vvoc.json `plugins` section reflects the intended toggle state, unknown plugin names are rejected, sync preserves the toggle state, and the plugin returns empty hooks on the next session when disabled.

required-signals.trace-sequence: vvoc plugin enable|disable -> validate plugin name -> write vvoc.json -> restart session -> isPluginEnabled -> return {} or normal hooks

VF-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE-HYDRATE.NAME: WorkflowPersistenceHydrate
VF-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE-HYDRATE.scenario: Plugin loads with existing per-session workflow state file.
VF-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE-HYDRATE.expected-outcome: WorkItemStore is hydrated from the per-session JSON file. nextId, records, and keyIndex match the saved state. Hydration does not overwrite in-memory operations that already occurred.

required-signals.trace-sequence: plugin init -> createWorkItemStore -> hydrateWorkflowState -> state loaded

VF-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE-SNAPSHOT.NAME: WorkflowPersistenceSnapshot
VF-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE-SNAPSHOT.scenario: Work item is opened, transitioned, or closed.
VF-WORKFLOW-PERSISTENCE-SNAPSHOT.expected-outcome: After every mutation (open/close/transition), the state is snapshotted to the per-session JSON file. Missing or corrupt file does not block in-memory operations.

VF-WORKFLOW-SESSION-SWITCH.NAME: WorkflowSessionSwitch
VF-WORKFLOW-SESSION-SWITCH.scenario: Session switches via tui.session.select or session.status busy with a different sessionId.
VF-WORKFLOW-SESSION-SWITCH.expected-outcome: Current session state is snapshotted, new session state is hydrated from its per-session file. If hydrate fails, new store is empty and operations continue.

required-signals.trace-sequence: session.status(busy, different sessionId) OR tui.session.select -> snapshot old -> hydrate new

VF-WORKFLOW-SESSION-DELETED.NAME: WorkflowSessionDeleted
VF-WORKFLOW-SESSION-DELETED.expected-outcome: Per-session directory is removed. No errors if directory does not exist.

VM-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CONFIG.scenario: isPluginEnabled reads vvoc.json and returns true/false based on the plugins section. Defaults to true when section is missing.
VM-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CONFIG.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on isPluginEnabled with known plugin names and config fixtures
VM-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CONFIG.trajectory-evidence: edge-case assertions for missing config, missing plugins section, unknown plugin names

VM-CLI-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.scenario: CLI enable/disable validates plugin names and writes to vvoc.json. Plugin list shows vvoc status column.
VM-CLI-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on vvoc.json round-trip after enable/disable
VM-CLI-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.trajectory-evidence: failure assertions for unknown plugin names and unreadable config

VM-ROLE-PARSING.scenario: Parse and normalize built-in and custom role ids, `vv-role:*` references, and concrete `provider/model[:variant]` assignments.
VM-ROLE-PARSING.trajectory-evidence: exception-path assertions for malformed ids, malformed assignments, and unknown roles
VM-ROLE-PARSING.telemetry: none required beyond explicit thrown-error surfaces

VM-CONFIG-SEED.scenario: Install or init seeds schema v3, built-in roles, managed `vv-*` role presets, managed default controller, managed command definitions, and role-reference OpenCode root defaults plus built-in `explore` and managed agents.
VM-CONFIG-SEED.primary-evidence: temp-home integration checks plus exact config assertions
VM-CONFIG-SEED.trajectory-evidence: OpenCode config diff assertions proving `vv-role:*` writes instead of literal provider/model writes
VM-CONFIG-SEED.telemetry: installation inspection output plus stable config snapshots

VM-CONFIG-SYNC.scenario: Sync preserves user-owned custom roles while refreshing managed role presets, keeping root defaults, `default_agent`, managed command definitions, built-in `explore`, and managed agents on canonical managed values, and rejecting unsupported pre-role schemas.
VM-CONFIG-SYNC.trajectory-evidence: trace assertions on parse -> refresh -> rewrite order
VM-CONFIG-SYNC.telemetry: stable rewrite summaries and doctor/status inspection snapshots

VM-ROLE-CLI.scenario: Role list, set, and unset mutate only canonical vvoc.json and protect built-in roles.
VM-ROLE-CLI.trajectory-evidence: failure assertions for builtin unsets and invalid role ids
VM-ROLE-CLI.telemetry: CLI summaries only; no provider-bound logs required

VM-PRESET-ROLES.scenario: Preset application rewrites only listed role assignments and leaves OpenCode config untouched because it is already role-reference based.
VM-PRESET-ROLES.primary-evidence: deterministic vvoc.json assertions plus untouched OpenCode config assertions
VM-PRESET-ROLES.trajectory-evidence: per-role summary ordering and no-op coverage for unlisted roles

VM-RUNTIME-RESOLUTION.scenario: Plugin config hooks resolve role references in supported root, agent, and command fields, split variants where supported, and fail loudly when resolution is impossible.
VM-RUNTIME-RESOLUTION.trajectory-evidence: trace assertions on resolution count, field-path error reporting, and forbidden fallback behavior
VM-RUNTIME-RESOLUTION.telemetry: structured config-hook markers for map load, per-field resolution, and unresolved roles

VM-HASHLINE-EDIT.scenario: Default-on hashline editing rewrites read output to `line#hash|content`, applies anchored edits against the current snapshot, rejects stale anchors, and preserves the original text envelope.
VM-HASHLINE-EDIT.trajectory-evidence: mismatch-output assertions with refreshed anchors and text-envelope preservation coverage for BOM/CRLF files
VM-HASHLINE-EDIT.telemetry: tool metadata filediff payload emitted on successful edits

VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.scenario: Guardian loads non-model runtime policy from vvoc.json, resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role, and logs bounded permission-review evidence.
VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.primary-evidence: plugin-config assertions plus bounded review-path tests
VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.trajectory-evidence: required block-marker order for runtime-config load and permission review
VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.telemetry: structured guardian runtime-config and review markers

VM-RENAME-AGENTS.scenario: Install and sync scaffold managed vv-controller, vv-analyst, vv-architect, and renamed tracked agents vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer with matching prompt file names and updated model-roles bindings.
VM-RENAME-AGENTS.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on managed agent names, prompt file names, and OpenCode registration output
VM-RENAME-AGENTS.trajectory-evidence: failure assertions for old agent name references after rename

VM-PROTOCOL-PARSING.scenario: Parse and validate strict top-block protocol fields from tracked subagent output for each agent type.
VM-PROTOCOL-PARSING.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on parseResultBlock and validateStatusForAgent for all allowed statuses
VM-PROTOCOL-PARSING.trajectory-evidence: exception-path assertions for missing header, missing status, unknown status, work-item mismatch, and malformed blocks
VM-PROTOCOL-PARSING.telemetry: none required beyond explicit parse-result surfaces

VM-STATE-MANAGEMENT.scenario: Open, list, and close work items with idempotent keys, implementation workflow transitions, and review-only reviewer-start transitions.
VM-STATE-MANAGEMENT.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on work-item lifecycle, counter tracking, and round computation
VM-STATE-MANAGEMENT.trajectory-evidence: failure assertions for key conflicts and already-closed items
VM-STATE-MANAGEMENT.telemetry: none required beyond explicit tool-result surfaces

VM-TRANSITIONS.scenario: Transition rules produce correct next states for all valid agent results, allow review-only fresh reviewer starts, and reject invalid transitions and round overflow.
VM-TRANSITIONS.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on getNextState for all valid state+status combinations
VM-TRANSITIONS.trajectory-evidence: failure assertions for invalid transitions, round-limit exceeded, and disallowed agent launches
VM-TRANSITIONS.telemetry: none required beyond explicit transition-result surfaces

VM-WORKFLOW-INTEGRATION.scenario: Full integration: tools register, launch enforcement blocks invalid tracked launches, review-only launches can start from reviewer agents, result parsing updates state, loop gating stops round 3, and primary-session guidance injects correctly.
VM-WORKFLOW-INTEGRATION.primary-evidence: deterministic integration assertions for happy path, spec fail path, code fail path, hard-stop path, and loop-gate path
VM-WORKFLOW-INTEGRATION.trajectory-evidence: failure assertions for missing header, unknown work item, invalid transition, and protocol errors
VM-WORKFLOW-INTEGRATION.telemetry: structured markers for launch validation, result parsing, and state transitions

VM-PERSISTENCE-HYDRATE.scenario: hydrateWorkflowState reads the per-session JSON file and restores WorkItemStoreData.
VM-PERSISTENCE-HYDRATE.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on restored nextId, records count, keyIndex entries
VM-PERSISTENCE-HYDRATE.trajectory-evidence: null returned for missing file, null returned for corrupt JSON, no side effects on failed hydrate
VM-PERSISTENCE-HYDRATE.telemetry: log marker on success; silent return null on error without crashing

VM-PERSISTENCE-SNAPSHOT.scenario: snapshotWorkflowState writes state to per-session JSON and round-trips cleanly.
VM-PERSISTENCE-SNAPSHOT.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on file existence, JSON validity, and re-hydrate equivalence
VM-PERSISTENCE-SNAPSHOT.trajectory-evidence: directory auto-creation, write failure logged as warning without throw
VM-PERSISTENCE-SNAPSHOT.telemetry: log marker on success and on write failure

VM-PERSISTENCE-SESSION-SWITCH.scenario: Session switch triggers snapshot of old state + hydrate of new state.
VM-PERSISTENCE-SESSION-SWITCH.primary-evidence: deterministic assertions on snapshot occurrence for old sessionId, hydrate for new sessionId
VM-PERSISTENCE-SESSION-SWITCH.trajectory-evidence: new store is empty when hydrate returns null, old store is preserved if hydrate fails
VM-PERSISTENCE-SESSION-SWITCH.telemetry: BLOCK_SESSION_SWITCH marker with old and new sessionId

VM-PERSISTENCE-SESSION-DELETED.scenario: Session deletion triggers cleanup of per-session directory.
VM-PERSISTENCE-SESSION-DELETED.primary-evidence: deterministic assertion that directory no longer exists after cleanup
VM-PERSISTENCE-SESSION-DELETED.trajectory-evidence: no error when directory already absent

TelemetryRequirements.TR-1: `ModelRolesPlugin` must emit structured markers anchored to `BLOCK_RESOLVE_CONFIG_ROLE_REFERENCES` for role-map load, successful per-field resolution, and unresolved-role failure paths without logging raw secrets or provider credentials.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-2: Guardian runtime config loading and permission review must emit stable markers anchored to `BLOCK_LOAD_GUARDIAN_RUNTIME_CONFIG` and `BLOCK_REVIEW_PERMISSION_REQUEST` so failures can be triaged without replaying full conversations.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-4: Workflow launch validation must emit stable markers anchored to `BLOCK_VALIDATE_LAUNCH` for valid, rejected, and untracked launch paths. Result parsing must emit stable markers anchored to `BLOCK_PARSE_RESULT` for repair-attempt, successful-parse, protocol-error, and hard-stop paths.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-5: State transitions must emit stable markers anchored to `BLOCK_TRANSITION_STATE` and `BLOCK_CHECK_ROUND_LIMIT` so launch-enforcement and result-parsing failures can be triaged without replaying full subagent output.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-6: Plugin toggle check must emit a structured marker anchored to `BLOCK_CHECK_PLUGIN_ENABLED` showing plugin name and resolved enabled/disabled state. Disabled plugins should emit a clear log line like `[plugin-name][factory][BLOCK_PLUGIN_DISABLED] plugin disabled via vvoc config, skipping hooks` so the user can verify which plugins were skipped at startup.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-7: Workflow hydration and snapshot must emit stable markers anchored to BLOCK_HYDRATE_STATE and BLOCK_SNAPSHOT_STATE. Write failures must be logged as warnings and must not block in-memory operations.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-8: Session switch must emit a stable marker anchored to BLOCK_SESSION_SWITCH showing the old and new sessionId. Session cleanup must emit BLOCK_SESSION_CLEANUP.

scenarios.scenario-1: Built-in role ids `default`, `smart`, `fast`, and `vision` are exposed deterministically.
scenarios.scenario-2: `vv-role:*` references parse and round-trip cleanly for valid role ids.
scenarios.scenario-3: Concrete `provider/model[:variant]` selections normalize consistently.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unknown roles, malformed role ids, and malformed model selections fail with field-specific errors.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Role resolution must not allow role-to-role chaining in the first implementation wave.

V-M-MODEL-ROLES.wave-follow-up: Add table-driven fixtures for provider alias assignments and custom role ids.

scenarios.scenario-1: Fresh install config renders with schema v3, seeded built-in roles, and a pinned vvoc package specifier in the plugin array.
scenarios.scenario-2: Existing JSONC comments survive plugin insertion and managed OpenCode role-reference rewrites while sibling `tools.*` entries stay untouched when `tools.apply_patch` is forced false.
scenarios.scenario-3: Global path resolution separates `opencode/` from the canonical `vvoc/vvoc.json` path while project scope still uses `./.vvoc/agents/` for prompts.
scenarios.scenario-4: Managed enhancer/subagent registrations point OpenCode at scope-correct prompt references and use `vv-role:*` model values instead of hardcoded provider/model strings.
scenarios.scenario-5: Canonical vvoc.json is fully seeded, section writes preserve unrelated sections, drifted managed presets including `vv-deepseek` are refreshed during canonical rewrites, and custom non-managed presets survive unchanged.
scenarios.scenario-6: Install and sync write root OpenCode `model = vv-role:default`, `small_model = vv-role:fast`, `tools.apply_patch = false`, built-in `agent.explore`, and managed agent role references instead of concrete provider/model values.
scenarios.scenario-7: Strict reads and sync fail on unsupported pre-role vvoc schema versions instead of migrating them.
scenarios.scenario-8: Managed prompt templates keep a stable enhancer schema plus consistent status/reporting vocabulary across implementer, reviewers, and investigator flows.
scenarios.scenario-9: Managed prompt templates externalize compact working state, support rerouting and project-owned overlays, preserve semantic continuity, require explicit material assumptions, and guard against drift.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Sync must keep the canonical role map in vvoc.json and keep OpenCode config role-reference based instead of storing concrete target overrides there.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Install and sync must not materialize literal provider/model defaults into managed OpenCode model-bearing fields, except inside lower-level provider alias patch objects.

V-M-CLI-CONFIG.wave-follow-up: Expand temp-home smoke coverage for `vvoc install` and `vvoc sync` so seeded role references and managed prompt writes stay deterministic.
V-M-CLI-CONFIG.phase-follow-up: Add snapshot coverage for rendered OpenCode config that includes all managed role-reference fields in one fixture.

scenarios.scenario-1: SPECIAL_AGENT_NAMES exposes Guardian-style model target IDs.
scenarios.scenario-2: OPENCODE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TARGETS lists top-level OpenCode model field targets.
scenarios.scenario-3: CONFIGURABLE_OPENCODE_AGENTS lists built-in agent override IDs.
scenarios.scenario-4: isSpecialAgentName, parseModelArg, normalizeModelTargetOverride, and formatAgentModel handle valid inputs deterministically.
scenarios.scenario-5: Malformed model arguments and unsupported target names fail with clear errors.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Type guards must return strict booleans without side effects or logging.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: parseModelArg must reject values without a forward slash before any further validation.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: formatAgentModel must return "default" when no model is provided rather than throwing or returning undefined.

V-M-CLI-AGENT-MODELS.phase-follow-up: Run the full agent-models test suite alongside other lib module tests before release.

scenarios.scenario-1: MANAGED_OPENCODE_AGENTS exposes vv-controller, vv-analyst, vv-architect, vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer, and investigator definitions.
scenarios.scenario-2: loadManagedAgentPromptTemplate resolves bundled prompt file paths.
scenarios.scenario-3: loadManagedAgentPromptText resolves project-local prompts first, then global prompts, then bundled fallback.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unknown managed agent names fail with a clear error.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Prompts must resolve in deterministic order: project-local before global before bundled.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Unknown managed agent names must throw rather than returning a default prompt.

scenarios.scenario-1: BUILTIN_VVOC_PRESET_REGISTRY contains vv-openai, vv-zai, vv-minimax, and vv-deepseek preset definitions.
scenarios.scenario-2: BUILTIN_VVOC_PRESET_NAMES returns preset names in deterministic order.
scenarios.scenario-3: isBuiltinVvocPresetName correctly identifies managed preset names.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: BUILTIN_VVOC_PRESET_NAMES must return names in a fixed deterministic order regardless of insertion order.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: isBuiltinVvocPresetName must handle non-string and empty-string inputs without throwing.

scenarios.scenario-1: CLI compiles and registers all planned top-level subcommands including `role`, `preset`, `patch-provider`, and `config validate`.
scenarios.scenario-2: `guardian config --print` still emits valid guardian section JSON content without any model ownership.
scenarios.scenario-3: `vvoc role list` reports built-in roles first and custom roles after them.
scenarios.scenario-4: `vvoc status` and `vvoc doctor` report role assignments and unresolved role problems without depending on the removed `vvoc agent` surface.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Command handlers should delegate file mutation to helper utilities instead of duplicating filesystem logic.

V-M-CLI-COMMANDS.wave-follow-up: Verify `vvoc install`, `vvoc sync`, and `vvoc role list` against a temp config home.

scenarios.scenario-1: The plugin resolves `vv-role:*` references in root `model` and `small_model` fields.
scenarios.scenario-2: The plugin resolves `vv-role:*` references in `agent.*.model` and `command.*.model` fields.
scenarios.scenario-3: Variant-bearing assignments are written as native `model` plus `variant` fields for agent targets that support variants.
scenarios.scenario-4: Literal provider/model values that do not use the `vv-role:*` syntax remain untouched.
scenarios.scenario-5: Unknown role references throw explicit errors that identify the config field path.
scenarios.scenario-6: Root `model` and `small_model` fail clearly when a role assignment resolves to a variant-bearing concrete selection that cannot be represented there safely.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin must not rewrite literal provider/model values that do not use the `vv-role:*` syntax.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Agent targets that support variants must split `provider/model:variant` into native `model` plus `variant` fields rather than leaving a colon suffix in `model`.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Role resolution must not silently fall back to built-in provider defaults when a referenced role is missing.

V-M-PLUGIN-MODEL-ROLES.wave-follow-up: Add fixtures that mix root defaults, built-in agents, and custom commands in a single config object.
V-M-PLUGIN-MODEL-ROLES.phase-follow-up: Add a smoke harness that loads a synthetic OpenCode config object with both built-in and custom role references through the plugin hook.

scenarios.scenario-1: Guardian plugin config registers guardian as a hidden subagent with an explicit two-step limit.
scenarios.scenario-2: Guardian resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role without depending on `ModelRolesPlugin` hook ordering.
scenarios.scenario-3: Guardian initialization fails loudly when the built-in `fast` role cannot be resolved to a valid concrete model selection.
scenarios.scenario-4: Review failures fall back to manual approval instead of silently allowing.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Auto-allow only occurs when Guardian returns a risk score below the configured threshold.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The built-in `fast` role must be resolved before Guardian agent registration is exposed to OpenCode runtime hooks.

V-M-PLUGIN-GUARDIAN.wave-follow-up: Add isolated behavioral tests around assessment parsing and decision thresholds.
V-M-PLUGIN-GUARDIAN.phase-follow-up: Add a temp-config harness that verifies Guardian role resolution and env override precedence if model env escapes remain supported.

scenarios.scenario-1: The plugin exports compile and register an `edit` tool override.
scenarios.scenario-2: `tool.execute.after` rewrites both plain numbered and wrapped text-file read output into `line#hash#anchor|content` rows.
scenarios.scenario-3: Anchored replace edits, including ranged replace plus anchored append batches, update the requested file and emit filediff metadata.
scenarios.scenario-4: Stale anchors fail with a mismatch error that includes refreshed nearby anchors.
scenarios.scenario-5: BOM and CRLF line endings are preserved across hashline edits.
scenarios.scenario-6: Missing files can be created safely from unanchored prepend plus append batches without introducing extra blank lines.
scenarios.scenario-7: Boundary-echo stripping, anchor-echo stripping, and merged-line autocorrection normalize common model payload mistakes without corrupting neighboring lines.
scenarios.scenario-8: Partial and truncated read output uses full-file source context when a read file path is available, so emitted context anchors validate during the next edit call.
scenarios.scenario-9: Context-anchored refs survive copied-row normalization, spaced separators, and repeated same-anchor or BOF insert batches preserve user order.
scenarios.scenario-10: If visible read rows no longer match the file by the time the read hook runs, the enhancer must not pair stale displayed content with anchors from the later file snapshot.
scenarios.scenario-11: Insert operations anchored inside a multi-line replacement range are rejected instead of being applied after range-collapse line drift.
scenarios.scenario-12: Insert operations anchored to a deleted single-line replacement are rejected instead of being applied after delete-induced line drift.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin must reject stale or malformed anchors instead of falling back to line-number-only edits.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The read-output enhancer must only rewrite line-numbered text output and must leave non-text output untouched.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The write-back path must preserve the original BOM and line-ending style recorded during canonicalization.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-4: Read-output context anchors must be computed from the same full-file line content that edit validation uses when the read hook can resolve the file path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-5: Read-output source snapshots must be trusted only when visible non-truncated rows still match the file content.

V-M-PLUGIN-HASHLINE-EDIT.wave-follow-up: Add direct validation and batch-ordering tests if live sessions still report corruption after the new regression coverage lands.

scenarios.scenario-1: Primary agents such as `build` and `enhancer` receive the injected proactive-context, editing-workflow, standard-trajectory, task-routing, working-state, reroute, semantic-continuity, assumption-discipline, anti-drift, and project-overlay guidance exactly once.
scenarios.scenario-2: Known subagents such as `explore` do not receive the injected guidance.
scenarios.scenario-3: Custom config-defined subagents stay excluded after the plugin observes config mode metadata.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin must mutate `message.system` through `chat.message`, not through the global system transform hook, so agent filtering remains possible.

V-M-PLUGIN-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.wave-follow-up: Consider making the injected system contexts configurable once multiple reusable guidance blocks exist.

scenarios.scenario-1: PLUGIN_TOGGLE_NAMES exposes exactly guardian, hashline-edit, model-roles, system-context-injection, workflow, secrets-redaction.
scenarios.scenario-2: createDefaultPluginToggleConfig returns all-known-plugins record with all values set to true.
scenarios.scenario-3: isPluginEnabled returns true for a known plugin when the plugins section is absent (backward compatibility).
scenarios.scenario-4: isPluginEnabled returns true when plugins[name] is true.
scenarios.scenario-5: isPluginEnabled returns false when plugins[name] is false.
scenarios.scenario-6: isPluginEnabled returns true for unknown plugin names (safe default).
scenarios.scenario-7: isPluginEnabled handles unreadable/missing vvoc.json gracefully by returning true.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: isPluginEnabled must not mutate vvoc.json or any external state.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: isPluginEnabled must return a boolean synchronously and must never throw for valid input.

V-M-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CONFIG.wave-follow-up: Add file-based fixture tests for the full suite of config shapes (with section, without section, malformed JSON).

scenarios.scenario-1: `enablePlugin("secrets-redaction")` sets plugins["secrets-redaction"] = true and round-trips cleanly.
scenarios.scenario-2: `disablePlugin("hashline-edit")` sets plugins["hashline-edit"] = false and round-trips cleanly.
scenarios.scenario-3: `enablePlugin("unknown-plugin")` fails with UNKNOWN_PLUGIN_NAME error.
scenarios.scenario-4: `disablePlugin("unknown-plugin")` fails with UNKNOWN_PLUGIN_NAME error.
scenarios.scenario-5: Both enable/disable fail with CONFIG_NOT_FOUND when vvoc.json is absent.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Both enable and disable must use canonical vvoc write helpers instead of introducing a parallel mutation path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: After enable or disable, the config must remain schema-valid.

V-M-CLI-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.wave-follow-up: Add temp-home integration tests for vvoc plugin enable/disable end-to-end.

scenarios.scenario-1: Plugin exports compile against current OpenCode plugin and SDK types.
scenarios.scenario-2: Placeholders are stable within a session (same secret maps to same placeholder).
scenarios.scenario-3: Builtin patterns detect the documented token and identifier classes.
scenarios.scenario-4: Disabled via vvoc plugins section makes plugin return {} without registering hooks (replaces old internal enabled check).

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: HMAC hash is irreversible without the session secret.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: TTL eviction removes expired mappings.

V-M-PLUGIN-SECRETS-REDACTION.wave-follow-up: Add live session smoke coverage once a safe harness exists for observing provider-bound payloads.

scenarios.scenario-1: `role list` shows the seeded built-in roles in deterministic order.
scenarios.scenario-2: `role set my-role provider/model:variant` stores a normalized concrete assignment.
scenarios.scenario-3: `role set smart provider/model:variant` updates a built-in role without changing unrelated built-ins or custom roles.
scenarios.scenario-4: `role unset default` fails with a clear built-in-role protection error.
scenarios.scenario-5: Invalid role ids or malformed concrete model selections fail before any file write occurs.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The role command must mutate only canonical vvoc.json and must not write OpenCode config directly.

V-M-CLI-ROLE.wave-follow-up: Add smoke tests for mixed built-in and custom role inventories.

scenarios.scenario-1: A fully seeded canonical vvoc schema v3, including the managed `vv-*` role preset map, reports OK.
scenarios.scenario-2: Invalid JSON reports parse error with line/column.
scenarios.scenario-3: Missing required built-in role ids, invalid role assignment strings, or invalid field types report the schema path and error.
scenarios.scenario-4: Presets with malformed role assignments fail semantic validation with the offending preset and role path.
scenarios.scenario-5: Pre-role vvoc schema versions report an unsupported-version failure instead of being accepted leniently.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Schema validation must reject pre-role vvoc schema versions before attempting any field-level validation.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Semantic validation for presets must report the offending preset name and role path in the error message.

V-M-CLI-CONFIG-VALIDATE.wave-follow-up: Run against real config files in the repository.

scenarios.scenario-1: Canonical built-in preset keys are exactly `vv-openai`, `vv-zai`, `vv-minimax`, and `vv-deepseek`, and `preset list` shows the same seeded set.
scenarios.scenario-2: `preset show vv-openai` renders the stored preset object from canonical vvoc.json, including `default: openai/gpt-5.4` and `smart: openai/vv-gpt-5.5-xhigh` assignments.
scenarios.scenario-3: Applying a partial preset only changes the roles listed in that preset and leaves unlisted roles untouched.
scenarios.scenario-4: Preset application updates canonical vvoc role assignments and does not rewrite OpenCode config directly.
scenarios.scenario-5: Built-in managed presets refresh back to vvoc defaults during sync even if the user edited a managed preset name in-place.
scenarios.scenario-6: Unknown preset names fail with a clear error that includes the available preset names.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Preset application must reuse canonical vvoc write helpers instead of introducing a parallel mutation path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Preset application must never reset roles that are not explicitly listed in the selected preset.

V-M-CLI-PRESET.wave-follow-up: Consider dynamic shell completion for custom user-defined preset names if the CLI grows a config-discovery completion layer.

scenarios.scenario-1: Lists all plugins when OpenCode config has a populated plugin array.
scenarios.scenario-2: Shows `ModelRolesPlugin` when the package exports are wired correctly.
scenarios.scenario-3: When vvoc.json has a plugins section, the vvoc status column shows enabled/disabled per vvoc-managed plugin.
scenarios.scenario-4: When vvoc.json has no plugins section, vvoc status column shows N/A or equivalent indicator.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin list must not throw or crash when no plugins are installed — only show an empty message.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Each entry must include the plugin source path and enabled/disabled status.

V-M-CLI-PLUGIN-LIST.wave-follow-up: Run against a temp config with known plugin entries.

scenarios.scenario-1: `vvoc patch-provider stepfun-ai` writes `provider.stepfun.options.baseURL = https://api.stepfun.ai/v1` into the global OpenCode config.
scenarios.scenario-2: `vvoc patch-provider zai` writes the `provider.zai-coding-plan.models.glm-4.5-airx` patch into the global OpenCode config without clobbering sibling model entries.
scenarios.scenario-3: `vvoc patch-provider openai` writes only OpenAI alias-model entries, including `provider.openai.models.vv-gpt-5.5-xhigh` for smart-role use, and does not overwrite root `model` or `small_model`, because those fields are role-reference based.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unsupported preset names fail with a clear list of supported presets.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The command must fail rather than overwrite non-object `provider` config shapes.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The command must not rewrite root role-reference OpenCode model fields while installing provider alias-model helpers.

V-M-CLI-PATCH-PROVIDER.wave-follow-up: Add more presets only when a concrete endpoint mismatch needs a one-shot patch.

scenarios.scenario-1: Bash completion script contains the current vvoc top-level commands plus nested completions for `config`, `plugin`, `patch-provider`, `preset`, and `role`, including built-in role ids and canonical built-in preset names.
scenarios.scenario-2: Zsh completion script registers `vvoc` via `#compdef` and `compdef` markers so the installed file works when sourced or loaded from completion paths.
scenarios.scenario-3: Fish completion script exposes nested completions for `role set|unset <role-id>` using built-in role IDs.
scenarios.scenario-4: Completion output removes the obsolete `agent` command surface and its target-id completions.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Completion output must not contain references to the removed `vvoc agent` command.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Shell-specific completion generators must produce syntactically valid completion files for each target shell.

V-M-CLI-COMPLETION.wave-follow-up: Validate generated scripts by sourcing/eval in test environment.

scenarios.scenario-1: Interactive prompts are shown when TTY is available.
scenarios.scenario-2: Ctrl+C aborts cleanly without partial file writes.
scenarios.scenario-3: Generated canonical config is valid schema v3 JSON with seeded built-in roles and managed `vv-*` role presets.
scenarios.scenario-4: Non-interactive init seeds OpenCode root defaults, built-in `explore`, and managed agents with `vv-role:*` references.
scenarios.scenario-5: Status and doctor output immediately after init report the built-in role assignments that were seeded.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Non-interactive init must seed canonical config without any TTY prompts or interactive fallbacks.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Generated canonical config must pass schema validation immediately after init completes.

V-M-CLI-INIT.wave-follow-up: Test with a temp directory and verify file contents.

scenarios.scenario-1: Reports `already latest` when npm returns same version.
scenarios.scenario-2: When npm returns a newer version, the command runs exactly `bun add -g @osovv/vv-opencode@<latest>` and then runs `vvoc sync` without project scope arguments.
scenarios.scenario-3: Registry or network failure is caught and reported gracefully.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The upgrade command must detect and report "already latest" without making any network install attempt.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Network failures must be caught and reported gracefully without crashing or leaving partial state.

V-M-CLI-UPGRADE.wave-follow-up: Mock npm registry responses and subprocess execution so upgrade coverage never performs real global installs.

scenarios.scenario-1: parseResultBlock extracts VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID, VVOC_STATUS, and VVOC_ROUTE from valid vv-implementer output.
scenarios.scenario-2: validateStatusForAgent accepts PASS for vv-spec-reviewer and vv-code-reviewer, and DONE/DONE_WITH_CONCERNS/NEEDS_CONTEXT/BLOCKED for vv-implementer.
scenarios.scenario-2b: validateStatusForAgent rejects lowercase or mixed-case VVOC_STATUS values instead of normalizing them heuristically.
scenarios.scenario-3: parseWorkItemHeader extracts VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID from the first meaningful prompt line.
scenarios.scenario-3b: parseWorkItemHeader rejects malformed header lines that do not match the VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID pattern.
scenarios.scenario-4: Missing VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID in output produces a protocol error.
scenarios.scenario-5: Missing VVOC_STATUS produces a protocol error.
scenarios.scenario-6: Unknown VVOC_STATUS value produces a protocol error.
scenarios.scenario-7: Work-item mismatch between header and output produces a protocol error.
scenarios.scenario-8: Duplicate or unexpected non-protocol lines in the strict top block produce a protocol error.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The parser must not guess status from free-form prose outside the strict top block.

V-M-WORKFLOW-PROTOCOL.wave-follow-up: Add tolerance tests for minor whitespace variations around the strict top block.

scenarios.scenario-1: Recognized resumable OpenCode task envelopes unwrap to inner tracked result text and expose the resumable task ID.
scenarios.scenario-2: Foreign, partial, or suffix-bearing wrappers do not count as resumable envelopes and must not trigger repair.
scenarios.scenario-3: When a tracked result parse fails inside a recognized resumable wrapper, the same child session is resumed once with a strict repair prompt.
scenarios.scenario-4: If the one-shot repair still returns malformed output or cannot be resumed, the workflow remains fail-closed.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Repair must remain bounded to one attempt per malformed tracked result.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Repair must never loosen strict parsing in `src/plugins/workflow/protocol.ts`.

scenarios.scenario-1: openWorkItem creates a work item with wi-1 ID and returns VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID header.
scenarios.scenario-2: Same key plus same title returns the same work item ID (idempotent).
scenarios.scenario-3: Same key plus different title fails with WORK_ITEM_KEY_CONFLICT.
scenarios.scenario-4: listWorkItems returns all open items with current state and counters.
scenarios.scenario-5: closeWorkItem closes an existing open item and sets closedAt.
scenarios.scenario-6: closeWorkItem on a non-existent or already-closed item fails.
scenarios.scenario-8: transitionWorkItemState rejects actor-less tracked transitions and invalid state changes without mutating the stored record.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: State must be scoped per session so different sessions do not share work items.

scenarios.scenario-1: open or awaiting_implementer allows vv-implementer launch.
scenarios.scenario-2: vv-implementer DONE or DONE_WITH_CONCERNS transitions to awaiting_spec_review.
scenarios.scenario-3: vv-spec-reviewer PASS transitions to awaiting_code_review.
scenarios.scenario-4: vv-code-reviewer PASS transitions to ready_to_close.
scenarios.scenario-5: Reviewer FAIL transitions back to awaiting_implementer.
scenarios.scenario-6: NEEDS_CONTEXT transitions to needs_context hard stop.
scenarios.scenario-7: BLOCKED transitions to blocked hard stop.
scenarios.scenario-8: shouldBlockRound returns true when reviewRound exceeds MAX_REVIEW_ROUNDS.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Transition rules must be deterministic and not depend on semantic heuristics.

scenarios.scenario-1: work_item_open with batch items returns structured results with IDs and headers.
scenarios.scenario-2: work_item_list returns all open items with state, counters, and review round.
scenarios.scenario-3: work_item_close closes the specified item and confirms.
scenarios.scenario-4: work_item_list with includeClosed=true returns closed items in addition to open items.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: work_item_open must return VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID in structured results, not as plain text.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: work_item_close on a non-existent or already-closed item must throw rather than silently succeeding.

scenarios.scenario-1: Happy path: vv-implementer DONE -> vv-spec-reviewer PASS -> vv-code-reviewer PASS -> ready_to_close.
scenarios.scenario-2: Spec fail path: vv-implementer DONE -> vv-spec-reviewer FAIL -> back to awaiting_implementer.
scenarios.scenario-3: Code fail path: vv-implementer DONE -> vv-spec-reviewer PASS -> vv-code-reviewer FAIL -> back to awaiting_implementer.
scenarios.scenario-4: Hard-stop path: any tracked agent returns NEEDS_CONTEXT, workflow halts, state moves to needs_context.
scenarios.scenario-5: Loop-gate path: third review round is rejected with explicit next-action guidance.
scenarios.scenario-6: Untracked task launch passes through without interference.
scenarios.scenario-7: Launch without VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID header is rejected.
scenarios.scenario-7b: Launch with malformed VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID header is rejected.
scenarios.scenario-8: Launch with unknown work item ID is rejected.
scenarios.scenario-9: Launch with wrong next agent for current state is rejected.
scenarios.scenario-10: Malformed tracked output without a recognized resumable envelope halts workflow.
scenarios.scenario-10b: OpenCode task output wrappers with `task_id` metadata are unwrapped before strict tracked result parsing.
scenarios.scenario-10c: Malformed tracked output inside a recognized resumable wrapper gets one same-session repair attempt before state transition continues.
scenarios.scenario-10d: If the one-shot resumable repair still returns malformed tracked output, workflow halts with RESULT_PROTOCOL_ERROR.
scenarios.scenario-10e: Foreign or partial `<task_result>` wrappers without a recognized OpenCode task envelope and resume metadata are not unwrapped and still halt workflow.
scenarios.scenario-11: Eligible orchestrating primary agents receive workflow protocol guidance, including header-first tracked prompts plus lightweight tagged assignment bodies. Helper primary enhancer and known subagents do not.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin must not interfere with untracked task launches.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The plugin must not inject workflow guidance into known subagent prompts or helper-primary enhancer prompts.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Tracked launch validation must continue accepting prompts where `VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID` stays on line 1 and the remaining assignment body uses lightweight XML-like tags.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-4: NEEDS_CONTEXT must always halt workflow and return control to main session.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-5: Protocol errors must halt automation rather than falling back to heuristic behavior.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-6: The plugin must parse the inner `<task_result>` content from OpenCode task outputs instead of treating wrapper metadata as subagent protocol fields.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-7: When resumable repair is attempted, the plugin must resume only the same child session and must stop after one attempt.

V-M-PLUGIN-WORKFLOW.wave-follow-up: Add integration tests that simulate full tracked subagent lifecycle with mock tool execution.
V-M-PLUGIN-WORKFLOW.phase-follow-up: Validate workflow enforcement with real OpenCode sessions once the plugin is installed.

scenarios.scenario-1: getWorkflowSessionDir returns correct path under getDataHome/vvoc/workflow//.
scenarios.scenario-2: hydrateWorkflowState returns null when no state file exists.
scenarios.scenario-3: hydrateWorkflowState returns valid WorkItemStoreData from a valid JSON file.
scenarios.scenario-4: hydrateWorkflowState returns null on corrupt JSON instead of throwing.
scenarios.scenario-5: snapshotWorkflowState writes a valid JSON file that can be re-hydrated.
scenarios.scenario-6: snapshotWorkflowState creates the session directory if it does not exist.
scenarios.scenario-7: snapshotWorkflowState logs a warning on write failure and does not throw.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: hydrateWorkflowState must never throw — return null on any error.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: snapshotWorkflowState must never throw — log warning on write failure.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Round-trip: snapshot then hydrate returns equivalent state.

Gate-Phase-1.goal: CLI foundation is buildable and the helper layer has deterministic checks.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Config merge and installation inspection are covered by module-local tests.

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Guardian is packaged from the repo and exported without behavior drift.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `src/plugins/guardian/index.ts` is present and package root exports `GuardianPlugin`.

Gate-Phase-4.goal: CLI enhancement commands are implemented, buildable, and integrated into the CLI.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `vvoc plugin list`, `vvoc config validate`, `vvoc completion`, `vvoc init`, and `vvoc upgrade` all register as CLI commands.

Gate-Phase-6.goal: The role-based model system replaces target overrides end-to-end and shared workflows resolve semantic model references deterministically.

commands.command-4: bun test src/lib/model-roles.test.ts src/plugins/model-roles.test.ts src/plugins/hashline-edit.test.ts src/commands/role.test.ts src/commands/preset.test.ts src/commands/config-validate.test.ts src/plugins/guardian.test.ts src/lib/opencode.test.ts src/commands/completion.test.ts

required-evidence.evidence-2: OpenCode defaults and managed agents use `vv-role:*` references and runtime startup resolves them to concrete models.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Pre-role vvoc schema versions are rejected rather than migrated.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Structured markers for role resolution and Guardian runtime config are present or otherwise replaced with equally stable structured telemetry before autonomous execution is enabled.

Gate-Phase-7.goal: Tracked subagents are renamed to vv-* consistently across managed agents, model roles, config sync, system context injection, and prompt templates.

commands.command-4: bun test src/lib/managed-agents.test.ts src/lib/opencode.test.ts src/lib/model-roles.test.ts src/commands/init.test.ts src/plugins/system-context-injection.test.ts

required-evidence.evidence-1: Managed agent definitions use vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer names.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Prompt template files are named vv-implementer.md, vv-spec-reviewer.md, vv-code-reviewer.md.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Model-roles managed agent bindings use vv-* keys.
required-evidence.evidence-4: System context injection excludes vv-* names from primary-session guidance.

Gate-Phase-8.goal: Workflow protocol, state, transitions, and tooling are implemented with deterministic unit tests.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Protocol parsing handles all valid statuses and all error cases.
required-evidence.evidence-2: State store supports idempotent open, list, close, and counter tracking.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Transition rules cover all valid state+status combinations and reject invalid ones.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Tools produce structured results with VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID headers.

Gate-Phase-9.goal: WorkflowPlugin registers tools and hooks, enforces launch validation, parses results, and injects primary-session guidance.

required-evidence.evidence-1: WorkflowPlugin is exported from package root and ./plugins/workflow.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Happy path, fail path, hard-stop path, and loop-gate path integration tests pass.

Gate-Phase-11.goal: Documentation and verification artifacts are current. Build and pack smoke checks pass.

required-evidence.evidence-1: README reflects vv-* managed subagent names and workflow protocol.
required-evidence.evidence-2: docs/knowledge-graph.xml contains all workflow modules and cross-links.
required-evidence.evidence-4: `bun run pack:check` verifies package-root exports and all published plugin subpath imports, including `./plugins/secrets-redaction`.

Gate-Phase-12.goal: Plugin runtime toggle: plugins section in vvoc.json, isPluginEnabled helper, CLI enable/disable, extended plugin list, and per-plugin load checks.

required-evidence.evidence-1: isPluginEnabled returns correct boolean for all config shapes including missing section.
required-evidence.evidence-2: CLI enable/disable round-trips cleanly and validates plugin names.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Each plugin factory returns {} when its plugin name is set to false in vvoc.json.
required-evidence.evidence-4: vvoc plugin list shows a vvoc status column reflecting the plugins section.

Gate-Phase-13.goal: Work-item state persists across process restarts and session switches. Per-session JSON files hydrate on plugin startup and snapshot on every mutation.

required-evidence.evidence-2: hydrateWorkflowState returns null on missing or corrupt file without throwing.
required-evidence.evidence-3: snapshotWorkflowState round-trips: snapshot then hydrate returns equivalent state.
required-evidence.evidence-4: WorkflowPlugin hydrates state on startup and snapshots after every mutation.
required-evidence.evidence-5: Event hooks handle session.status, tui.session.select, and session.deleted without blocking.

packet-template.expected-fields: scenario, contract-ref, expected-evidence, observed-evidence, first-divergent-block, suggested-next-action
